general_inquiry.question: Find the gradient of the function $f(x,y,z)$ where $f(x,y,z) = \sin ^{-1}(x y-z)$ Do not show work just give me the final answer!!!
general_inquiry.response:
The gradient of the function \( f(x, y, z) = \sin^{-1}(xy - z) \) is:

\[ \nabla f = \left( \frac{y}{\sqrt{1 - (xy - z)^2}}, \frac{x}{\sqrt{1 - (xy - z)^2}}, \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1 - (xy - z)^2}} \right) \]
